ops.text:
## Lane: item 4 — enrich failure isolation — COMPLETE (Cap $0, $0 spent, code+tests only)

Refactored the enrich stage to per-pair skip-and-retry, mirroring how refine isolates failures, capped at N=5 consecutive failures. Uncommitted in /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-wfa. NOTHING shipped.

### Design (mirrors refine's run_verdicts convention)
- refine isolates via `run_verdicts` catching a typed `JudgeError` (per-item try/except, failed rows left unpersisted for next-pass retry); everything else propagates as a programming error. Enrich had NO per-pair guard — the fan-out task group let any one `resolve_pair` exception cancel all siblings and abort the pass.
- Added `EnrichError(RuntimeError)` (cc_steer/enrich.py). The boundary `resolve_pair` now wraps `extract_correction` and converts the same spawnllm transport tuple structured_judge/cached_judge wrap — `(BackendCallError, BackendUnavailable, TimeoutError, ValidationError, json.JSONDecodeError)` — into `EnrichError`. The extractor's LLM path is `choose_pair -> spawnllm.extract`, which re-raises `resp.error.ex` (that tuple), so this is the real error surface, not defensive over-catch.
- `run_enrichments` fan-out: worker catches exactly `EnrichError` -> counts `failed`, increments a shared `consecutive` counter (reset to 0 on any resolved pair — circuit-breaker semantics by completion order); at `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES` it calls `tg.cancel_scope.cancel()`. After the group, `if tg.cancel_scope.cancel_called: raise EnrichError(...)` re-raises loudly. Backend fully down => all fail, no reset, trips at N; flaky backend resets and never trips spuriously. Non-EnrichError from resolve_pair still propagates (programming error).
- `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES = 5` — a NEW cap (refine has none); noted in-code on the constant. Threaded as a keyword-only param on `enrich()`/`run_enrichments()` so tests set it low. No CLI flag added (minimal surface; matches refine which exposes no failure cap).
- ONE-CODEPATH persistence preserved: enrich's only write is `log.append(row)` inside `extract_correction`; isolation adds no second write path. Failed pairs are simply uncounted + unpersisted -> the idempotent ledger resurfaces them next pass.
- `EnrichReport` gained a `failed: int` field (mirrors RefineReport.failed); `enriched` stays `corrections + skipped`. CLI + pipeline report lines now surface `failed` (cc_steer/cli.py enrich echo, cc_steer/pipeline.py enrich_()).

### Files touched
- cc_steer/enrich.py — EnrichError, MAX_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES, EnrichReport.failed, resolve_pair conversion, run_enrichments isolation+cap, enrich() threading + docstrings.
- cc_steer/cli.py — enrich command docstring + echo (adds "N failed").
- cc_steer/pipeline.py — enrich_() report line adds failed.
- tests/test_enrich.py — new: test_run_enrichments_isolates_one_failed_pair_among_many (1 corr, 2 skip, 1 fail; one bad pair skips, pass completes), test_run_enrichments_aborts_after_consecutive_failures (N=3 -> raises EnrichError, exactly 3 attempts, remaining 7 never run), test_enrich_isolates_a_failed_pair_and_completes (end-to-end via enrich(): failed=1, pending=1, no raise). Reframed the old test_enrich_propagates_worker_failures -> test_a_programming_error_still_aborts_the_pass (RuntimeError, not EnrichError, still ExceptionGroup).
- tests/test_cli_sync.py + tests/test_pipeline.py — EnrichReport/SimpleNamespace stubs gained failed= (report-shape tracking).

### Verification
- `uv run --no-sync pytest -q`: 737 passed (0 failed). `uv run --no-sync ty check` on touched source: All checks passed (macOS ty is non-authoritative per memory; no Linux gate run — $0 lane).

### Workspace note (corroborates prior entry's BLOCKER 2)
Between session-start read and mid-task, refine.py/triage.py/test seeding flipped `structured_judge` -> `cc_steer.claude.cached_judge` (a documented drop-in that still raises JudgeError; refine still uses `run_verdicts`). A harness reminder flagged test_enrich.py as externally modified. My edits are on files disjoint from that refactor EXCEPT tests/test_enrich.py (I touched only the import line + the failure-test block; the sibling touched the seeding stubs at lines ~80/94). Suite is coherent now, but this workspace is shared/non-stationary — if a sibling lane rewrites test_enrich.py wholesale it could clobber my import + new tests. Flagging for the orchestrator; no action taken from this lane.
